title: Restart a Robot From the Restart Panel
summary: Use the documented restart panel to select a robot by ID or selection control, click Restart, allow the restart cycle to complete, and verify map visibility when the robot is positioned at a restart point or QR code cell.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to restart a selected robot from the restart panel and confirm whether it returns to the map after restart.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to conclude a restart failed solely because the robot does not appear on the map immediately after restart.
Do not use this procedure as a complete recovery method when the robot is not positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the robot in the restart panel, initiate the restart, allow the restart cycle to complete, and verify map visibility based on the documented positioning condition.

safety_notes: Map visibility after restart depends on the robot being positioned so it can identify a label or QR code cell.
If a robot is stopped between labels, it may not snap back onto the map automatically after restart because it cannot see a label.
Further recovery may be required if the positioning condition is not met or the robot does not return to the map.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the robot restart panel
Robot ID or robot selection control

access_required: Access to the robot restart panel
system_preconditions: The robot to be restarted can be identified by robot ID or selected in the panel.
For map verification after restart, the robot should be positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell as described by the source.

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame showing the restart robot panel workflow, including robot ID entry or robot selection, Restart control, and map display expectation after restart.

steps.title: Open the restart panel and identify robot selection
steps.purpose: Locate the documented interface used to restart a selected robot.
steps.instruction: Open the robot restart panel and identify the area where you can enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot to restart.
steps.expected_result: The restart panel is open and the robot selection area is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel shows the robot ID entry or robot selection control and the Restart control is available on the panel.
steps.failure_condition: The restart panel or robot selection area cannot be identified from the available interface.

actions.label: Locate restart panel
actions.exact_action: Open the robot restart panel and identify the robot ID entry or robot selection control.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart Robot panel showing robot ID entry or robot selection control and the Restart button.

stop_or_escalate_if: The restart panel cannot be accessed.
The robot selection area cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you put in the ID or you select the robot that you're interested in restarting. You press the restart button on this panel over here'. OCR slide: 'Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot' and 'Click Restart'.

steps.title: Select the robot to restart
steps.purpose: Identify the specific robot that will receive the restart command.
steps.instruction: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot you want to restart.
steps.expected_result: The intended robot is selected in the restart panel.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct robot is entered or selected in the panel.
steps.failure_condition: The intended robot cannot be entered or selected.

actions.label: Enter or select robot
actions.exact_action: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot selection area where the operator enters the corresponding robot ID or selects the robot.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct robot cannot be identified or selected in the panel.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR slide: 'Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot'.
Transcript: 'you put in the ID or you select the robot that you're interested in restarting.'

steps.title: Click Restart
steps.purpose: Initiate the restart for the selected robot.
steps.instruction: Click the Restart control on the panel.
steps.expected_result: The restart command is issued to the selected robot.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel accepts the Restart action for the selected robot.
steps.failure_condition: The Restart action cannot be initiated from the panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart control on the restart panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Restart control is unavailable or does not respond.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'You press the restart button on this panel over here'.

steps.title: Allow the robot to restart
steps.purpose: Give the robot time to complete the documented restart cycle.
steps.instruction: Allow the robot to go through its restart cycle.
steps.expected_result: The robot proceeds through the restart cycle.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot restarts as described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear to complete the restart cycle.
stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not proceed through the restart cycle.

steps.title: Check robot positioning for map visibility
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the documented positioning condition for map display is met.
steps.instruction: Check whether the robot is positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell, as described by the source.
steps.expected_result: You know whether the robot meets the documented positioning condition for appearing on the map after restart.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell, or otherwise can identify a label/QR code as described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is between labels or otherwise not positioned so it can identify a label or QR code cell.

actions.label: Verify positioning condition
actions.exact_action: Check whether the robot is at a restart point or on the QR code cell.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot is not at a restart point or on the QR code cell.
The robot is stopped between labels and cannot see a label.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR slide references a restart point or QR code cell and states: 'After starting, the robot that has been positioned will be displayed on the map.'
Transcript: 'if your robot is stopped for some reason in between two labels and you perform a restart, it will not snap back onto the map automatically... It's because it can't see a label.'

steps.title: Verify the robot appears on the map
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented post-restart outcome in the map display.
steps.instruction: Verify that after starting, the positioned robot is displayed on the map.
steps.expected_result: The restarted robot is displayed on the map when the documented positioning condition is met.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot appears on the map after restart when positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell.
steps.failure_condition: The robot does not appear on the map after restart, especially if it cannot identify a label or QR code cell.

actions.label: Check map display
actions.exact_action: Verify that after starting, the positioned robot is displayed on the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map display expectation after restart for a robot that has been positioned correctly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot does not return to the map after restart.
The positioning condition is not met and further recovery is required.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR slide: 'After starting, the robot that has been positioned will be displayed on the map.'

success_criteria: The intended robot is selected from the restart panel.
The Restart action is initiated from the panel.
The robot completes its restart cycle.
When positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell, the robot is displayed on the map after starting.
healthy_conditions: The restart panel shows robot selection input and Restart control.
The selected robot accepts the restart action.
The robot appears on the map after restart when positioned so it can identify the required label or QR code cell.
failure_conditions: The restart panel or robot selection area cannot be accessed or identified.
The intended robot cannot be selected.
The Restart action cannot be initiated.
The robot does not complete the restart cycle.
The robot is between labels or otherwise cannot identify a label or QR code cell after restart.
The robot does not appear on the map after restart.
escalation_guidance: If the robot does not appear on the map after restart, do not assume the restart failed; the source states map visibility depends on label or QR code recognition.
If the robot is not positioned at a restart point or on the QR code cell, or if it is between labels and cannot see a label, further recovery may be required.
Escalate when the documented positioning condition is not met or the robot does not return to the map after restart.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Restart Robot panel workflow and post-restart map display expectation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'you put in the ID or you select the robot that you're interested in restarting. You press the restart button on this panel over here'. OCR slide: 'Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot' and 'Click Restart'.
OCR slide: 'Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot', 'Click Restart', 'The robot will restart', and 'After starting, the robot that has been positioned will be displayed on the map.'

The source does not specify role boundaries beyond operator-oriented training language.
